title: Enable OpenBao UI in kind overlay and document access
description:
# GitHub Issue #246: Enable the OpenBao UI in the kind overlay and document access in the Quick Start

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Flip `ui = true` in the standalone server config produced by the kind overlay patch (`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:42`) and add a Quick Start step analogous to "Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI" (`docs/quick-start.md:150`) that walks through port-forwarding to the OpenBao service and logging in with the root token already stashed in the `openbao-init-keys` Secret. The listener is already TLS-terminated via cert-manager on `:8200` (`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:44-50`), so the UI is reachable at `https://localhost:8200/ui/` after `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200`. Token retrieval is a one-liner that is already documented in `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md:170-171`: `kubectl get secret openbao-init-keys -n openbao-system -o jsonpath='{.data.init-output}' | base64 -d | jq -r '.root_token'`. The production HelmRelease (`deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:43`) keeps `ui = false` unchanged — the flip is intentionally scoped to the kind overlay only, mirroring the kind-only posture of Headlamp (see the header comment in `deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml:12-15`).

Upstream docs for the toggle: https://openbao.org/docs/configuration/ui/. Requirements are minimal — a single `ui = true` top-level key plus at least one listener (which we already have).

**Motivation**: The Quick Start already teaches users to open Headlamp so they can watch Keystone, MariaDB, Memcached, and ExternalSecret reconciliation live. But OpenBao is where the bootstrap secrets, KV paths, auth mounts, and policies written by `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-*.sh` actually land — and without the UI there is no low-friction way to verify "did `keystone-admin` really get written to `openstack/keystone-admin`?", "is the Kubernetes auth mount configured correctly?", "does the policy I just wrote grant the paths I expect?". Today the only inspection path is `kubectl exec -it openbao-0 -- bao kv get ...`, which does not work for first-time users who do not yet know the path names. The UI collapses that discovery step into clicking through the tree. It also improves the story for the bootstrap walkthrough in `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md`, because readers can click along with each step instead of copy-pasting `bao` CLI invocations. The change is a one-line config flip plus a Quick Start section — roughly the same shape and cost as Headlamp's "Step 4".

**Affected Areas**:
- `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` (flip to `ui = true` in the standalone server config patch at line 42)
- `docs/quick-start.md` (new section "Step 4b — Open the OpenBao UI" or renumbered alongside Headlamp, covering port-forward + root-token login + the self-signed cert warning)
- `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md` (cross-link the new Quick Start UI step near the "Running the Full Bootstrap" / root-token export block at line 164-171)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` sets `ui = true` for the OpenBao standalone listener; production HelmRelease at `deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:43` stays `ui = false`
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster ends with an OpenBao pod that serves `GET /ui/` on `:8200` with HTTP 200 (or a 307 redirect to `/ui/vault/`)
- [ ] The Quick Start gains a step analogous to "Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI" containing (1) the `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200` command (exact svc name verified against the rendered chart), (2) the root-token extraction one-liner from `openbao-init-keys`, (3) the `https://localhost:8200/ui/` URL, and (4) an explicit note that the browser will show a self-signed certificate warning because OpenBao's TLS is issued by the internal cert-manager CA
- [ ] The Quick Start section points readers at `docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md` for deeper "what does each KV path / auth mount / policy mean" material rather than duplicating it
- [ ] The "Architecture snapshot" pod/UI list in the Quick Start (around `docs/quick-start.md:131`) mentions the OpenBao UI the same way it already mentions Headlamp
- [ ] No change to the OpenBao `HelmRepository` chart version pin (`deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:23`) — the UI flip is a server-config change, not a chart upgrade
- [ ] CI e2e in kind still passes: `make test-e2e` and the chaos job that depends on `tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh` must not regress because of the extra listener route

**Non-Goals**:
- Enabling the OpenBao UI in production clusters (`deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml`). That is a separate discussion about whether an authenticated UI fits the project's production posture, and should be preceded by a conversation about OIDC/SSO integration before flipping it on.
- Exposing the UI through an Ingress or LoadBalancer in kind. Port-forward is consistent with how the Quick Start already reaches Headlamp and the Keystone API (`docs/quick-start.md:423`).
- Replacing the `bao` CLI bootstrap scripts in `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/` with UI-driven steps. The scripts remain the source of truth for `make deploy-infra`; the UI is purely an inspection aid.

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/246

stories.title: Developer explores OpenBao KV paths and auth mounts from the browser in kind
stories.role: forge developer running the Quick Start on a local kind cluster
stories.want: the OpenBao web UI to be reachable on https://localhost:8200/ui/ after a single port-forward, using the root token already stored in openbao-init-keys
stories.so_that: I can click through the secret tree (openstack/keystone-admin, openstack/keystone-db, mariadb-root-password), inspect the kubernetes auth mount and policies, and verify the bootstrap scripts wrote what they claim — without memorizing bao CLI paths
criteria: With a fresh kind cluster after make deploy-infra, kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200 succeeds and binds on localhost:8200
Opening https://localhost:8200/ui/ in a browser renders the OpenBao login page (HTTP 200 or 307 → /ui/vault/), not a 404 or connection refused
Pasting the token from `kubectl get secret openbao-init-keys -n openbao-system -o jsonpath='{.data.init-output}' | base64 -d | jq -r '.root_token'` logs in successfully with root-level access
The secret tree visible in the UI contains the openstack/ KV v2 mount with keystone-admin, keystone-db, and mariadb-root-password entries written by write-bootstrap-secrets.sh
The Auth Methods page shows the kubernetes/management mount used by the ClusterSecretStore

stories.title: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
stories.role: first-time user working through docs/quick-start.md end-to-end
stories.want: a Step 4b (or renumbered) that walks through opening the OpenBao UI with the same structure as Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI
stories.so_that: I can follow a single consistent pattern (wait → token → port-forward → browser URL) rather than having to piece the procedure together from the bootstrap reference doc
criteria: The Quick Start contains a numbered step titled along the lines of 'Open the OpenBao UI' placed adjacent to Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI
The step shows the exact kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200 command (svc name verified against the rendered chart)
The step shows the root-token extraction one-liner: kubectl get secret openbao-init-keys -n openbao-system -o jsonpath='{.data.init-output}' | base64 -d | jq -r '.root_token'
The step shows the https://localhost:8200/ui/ URL (https, not http)

The step includes an explicit note that the browser will warn about a self-signed certificate because OpenBao's TLS is issued by the internal cert-manager CA, with guidance on how to proceed (accept the risk in a throwaway profile)
The step links to docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md for deeper material on KV paths, auth mounts, and policies instead of duplicating it
The 'Architecture snapshot' block around docs/quick-start.md:131 lists the OpenBao UI the same way it lists Headlamp (kind-only demo, not waited on)

stories.title: Platform engineer is protected against accidentally enabling the UI in production
stories.role: platform engineer reviewing changes to Flux manifests
stories.want: the kind overlay to flip ui = true while the production HelmRelease keeps ui = false, with the kind-only scope documented the same way Headlamp is
stories.so_that: a merge of this PR cannot ship an unauthenticated root-token UI into production clusters
criteria: deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml line 42 reads `ui = true` inside the standalone server config patch
deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml line 43 still reads `ui = false` inside the HA raft config (unchanged)
The OpenBao HelmRepository chart version pin at deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:23 is NOT modified

The kind overlay kustomization.yaml has a short inline comment near the ui = true line clarifying the flip is kind-only (mirroring the comment pattern in headlamp.yaml:12-15), referencing feature CC-0082

stories.title: CI keeps passing: infra-stack-health, chaos unseal, and deploy-infra all green
stories.want: make test-e2e and the chaos job depending on tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh to remain green after the UI flip
stories.so_that: the ui = true toggle is verified to be a no-op for the StatefulSet readiness, Raft/unseal flow, and ExternalSecret sync that CI already asserts
criteria: tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml continues to pass without modification (OpenBao StatefulSet readyReplicas ≥ 1)
tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh does not regress (no new listener binding conflict, no change to unseal key handling)
make deploy-infra on a fresh kind cluster still completes within the existing HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT / POD_TIMEOUT budgets
A reproducible smoke check against the running kind cluster: `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` after port-forward returns HTTP 200 or 307

requirements.description: The kind overlay SHALL set `ui = true` inside the OpenBao standalone server config patch at deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml (replacing the existing `ui = false` at line 42), with no other change to the HCL config block (listener, storage, service_registration blocks unchanged).
requirements.rationale: This is the single functional change that enables the OpenBao UI in kind; any collateral edit to the HCL would risk breaking the already-working TLS listener, Raft storage, and service registration.

scenarios.name: Rendered kind manifest contains ui = true
scenarios.when: kustomize build deploy/kind/base is executed
scenarios.then: the rendered HelmRelease named openbao in namespace openbao-system contains `ui = true` inside spec.values.server.standalone.config
and_then: the rendered config still contains the listener "tcp" block with tls_disable = 0, address [::]:8200, tls_cert_file /openbao/tls/tls.crt, tls_key_file /openbao/tls/tls.key
the rendered config still contains storage "raft" with path /openbao/data
the rendered config still contains service_registration "kubernetes" {}

scenarios.name: Production HelmRelease is untouched
scenarios.when: kustomize build deploy/flux-system is executed
scenarios.then: the rendered HelmRelease named openbao still contains `ui = false` inside spec.values.server.ha.raft.config
and_then: deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml line 43 is unchanged in git diff
chart version pin `>=0.5.0 <1.0.0` at deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:23 is unchanged

scenarios.name: Kind-only scope is documented inline
scenarios.when: a reviewer reads the kind overlay patch
scenarios.then: a short comment next to `ui = true` states the flip is kind-only and references the feature ID CC-0082
and_then: the comment style mirrors the kind-only comment block in deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml:12-15

requirements.description: After make deploy-infra on a fresh kind cluster, the OpenBao pod SHALL serve GET /ui/ on :8200 with HTTP 200 or a 307 redirect to /ui/vault/, using the TLS certificate already mounted from the openbao-tls Secret.
requirements.rationale: This is the observable verification that ui = true took effect; without it the toggle is silent.

scenarios.name: UI endpoint responds over TLS
scenarios.when: kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200 is running and `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` is executed
scenarios.then: the response is HTTP 200 or 307
and_then: the TLS handshake completes using the openbao-tls certificate signed by the internal selfsigned-cluster-issuer

scenarios.name: API endpoint continues to work
scenarios.when: curl -k https://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health is executed against the same port-forward
scenarios.then: the response is HTTP 200 with a JSON body including initialized=true and sealed=false
and_then: the existing ClusterSecretStore still reports status Ready=True reason=Valid

scenarios.name: Root token login succeeds
scenarios.when: the token from `kubectl get secret openbao-init-keys -n openbao-system -o jsonpath='{.data.init-output}' | base64 -d | jq -r '.root_token'` is posted to /v1/auth/token/lookup-self
scenarios.then: the response is HTTP 200 with policies=["root"]
and_then: the token renders the openstack/ KV v2 mount and its keystone-admin, keystone-db, mariadb-root-password entries

requirements.description: docs/quick-start.md SHALL gain a new step analogous to Step 4 — Open the Headlamp UI covering (1) the kubectl port-forward command, (2) the root-token extraction one-liner, (3) the https://localhost:8200/ui/ URL, and (4) an explicit self-signed certificate warning note.
requirements.rationale: The acceptance criteria in the feature description require these four elements verbatim. The step must be self-contained so readers do not have to jump to the bootstrap reference to log in.

scenarios.name: Step contains all four required elements
scenarios.when: a reader follows the Quick Start top-to-bottom
scenarios.then: they encounter a section between Step 4 (Headlamp) and Step 5 (Deploy the Keystone operator) that contains all four elements (port-forward, token one-liner, URL, TLS warning)
and_then: the kubectl port-forward command uses the exact service name `svc/openbao` in namespace `openbao-system` on port 8200:8200
the URL shown is https://localhost:8200/ui/ (HTTPS, not HTTP)
the token one-liner exactly matches the form used in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md:170-171

scenarios.name: Service name is verified against the rendered chart
scenarios.when: the planner/implementer runs `helm template` (or reads the openbao chart values) to confirm the headless/client svc name
scenarios.then: the command in the Quick Start matches the actual Service that exposes 8200/TCP with ClusterIP (not the openbao-internal headless service used for Raft peer discovery)
and_then: a brief inline hint in the Quick Start mentions that svc/openbao is the client service (not svc/openbao-internal)

scenarios.name: Step cross-links to deeper reference
scenarios.when: a reader wants to understand what each KV path / auth mount / policy means
scenarios.then: the Quick Start step points them at docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md instead of duplicating that content
and_then: the link target is a deep link to a section of the bootstrap reference, not just the page root, so the reader lands on the KV/auth/policy material

requirements.description: The Quick Start SHALL explicitly warn readers that the browser will show a self-signed-certificate warning because OpenBao's TLS is issued by the internal cert-manager CA, and SHALL tell them how to proceed safely.
requirements.rationale: Without this note, first-time readers hit a browser warning with no context, assume the step is broken, and abandon the walkthrough. This is the single most common friction point for self-signed TLS in kind demos.

scenarios.name: Warning note is present and specific
scenarios.when: a reader reaches the https://localhost:8200/ui/ step
scenarios.then: the note names the self-signed cert condition, identifies the internal cert-manager CA (selfsigned-cluster-issuer) as the issuer, and tells the reader to click through in a throwaway browser profile
and_then: the note is formatted as a tip/note callout consistent with other Quick Start callouts (e.g. ::: tip / > **Note:**)

scenarios.name: No instruction to disable TLS verification globally
scenarios.when: a reader follows the note
scenarios.then: they are not told to disable system-wide TLS verification or to install the internal CA into their system trust store
and_then: the note frames the click-through as kind-local and scoped to this demo session

scenarios.name: Optional curl -k guidance is consistent
scenarios.when: the verification step uses curl
scenarios.then: the -k flag is shown with a one-line rationale that matches the browser warning note, so both paths are consistent

requirements.description: The 'Architecture snapshot' pod/UI list around docs/quick-start.md:131 SHALL mention the OpenBao UI in the same manner it already mentions Headlamp — as a kind-only demo UI surface served by an existing pod and not waited on by make deploy-infra.
requirements.rationale: The snapshot is the reader's single mental model of 'what is running'; omitting the UI hides a surface that now exists and leaves the snapshot stale.

scenarios.name: UI surface is listed alongside Headlamp
scenarios.when: a reader reads the ::: details What gets deployed block
scenarios.then: a row or bullet identifies the OpenBao UI on svc/openbao :8200 in namespace openbao-system as a kind-only surface
and_then: the description matches Headlamp's phrasing style (kind-only demo UI, not waited on)

scenarios.name: Snapshot does not duplicate UI access steps
scenarios.when: the snapshot references the UI
scenarios.then: it points to the new Quick Start step for how to reach it, rather than restating the port-forward and token commands

requirements.description: docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md SHALL cross-link the new Quick Start UI step near the 'Running the Full Bootstrap' / root-token export block (around lines 164-171), so bootstrap readers can jump to the click-through walkthrough.
requirements.rationale: The bootstrap reference is where readers land when inspecting OpenBao; a two-way link prevents duplication and makes the UI discoverable from both directions.

scenarios.name: Bootstrap doc gains a cross-link
scenarios.when: a reader scrolls through the 'Running the Full Bootstrap' section
scenarios.then: they see a short pointer to the Quick Start step for opening the OpenBao UI in kind
and_then: the cross-link notes that the UI is kind-only, so production readers know not to expect it

scenarios.name: No content is duplicated
scenarios.when: the cross-link is added
scenarios.then: the bootstrap reference does not gain its own copy of the port-forward / token-extraction / browser-warning instructions
and_then: the Quick Start remains the single source of truth for the UI walkthrough

requirements.description: The change SHALL NOT modify the OpenBao HelmRepository chart version pin (deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:23) nor any production HelmRelease value.
requirements.rationale: The feature is a kind-local config toggle, not a chart upgrade; scope discipline protects production from collateral drift.

scenarios.name: Chart version pin is unchanged
scenarios.when: git diff is inspected on the PR
scenarios.then: deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml is either absent from the diff or, if touched, the version field remains `>=0.5.0 <1.0.0`

scenarios.name: Production values are unchanged
scenarios.when: git diff is inspected on the PR
scenarios.then: no key under spec.values in deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml has been modified
and_then: ui = false in the ha.raft.config block is unchanged

requirements.description: Existing CI coverage SHALL continue to pass: tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml and the chaos unseal path (tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh) SHALL not regress.
requirements.rationale: The UI listener lives on the same :8200 address as the API, so a misconfiguration could silently break ExternalSecret sync or the unseal flow. The feature is only safe if existing assertions still hold.

scenarios.name: Infra stack health passes
scenarios.when: chainsaw test tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health is run against a fresh kind cluster with the UI flip
scenarios.then: OpenBao StatefulSet readyReplicas >= 1 and all ExternalSecrets reach status Ready=True reason=SecretSynced
and_then: ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store remains status Ready=True reason=Valid

scenarios.name: Chaos unseal script does not regress
scenarios.when: tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh is invoked after a simulated pod kill
scenarios.then: the script unseals the pod using the existing 3-of-5 threshold without new errors
and_then: no extra listener-binding errors appear in the OpenBao pod logs

scenarios.name: deploy-infra wait conditions unchanged
scenarios.when: make deploy-infra runs on a fresh kind cluster
scenarios.then: the existing HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT and POD_TIMEOUT budgets are sufficient (no new wait step is added)

tasks.title: Flip ui = false → ui = true in deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml (line 42) inside the openbao standalone HelmRelease patch, with an inline comment CC-0082 noting the flip is kind-only (REQ-001, REQ-007)

Run `kustomize build deploy/kind/base | yq 'select(.kind == "HelmRelease" and .metadata.name == "openbao") | .spec.values.server.standalone.config'` and confirm the rendered config contains `ui = true` and still contains the listener/storage/service_registration blocks untouched (REQ-001)

Render the production flux-system overlay (`kustomize build deploy/flux-system`) and confirm deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml is untouched — ui = false in ha.raft.config, chart version pin unchanged (REQ-007)

Deploy the flipped overlay on a fresh kind cluster: `kind create cluster --config hack/kind-config.yaml` then `make deploy-infra`; confirm openbao-0 reaches Running and init/unseal succeeds with no new pod-log errors (REQ-002, REQ-008)

Smoke-check the UI endpoint: in one terminal `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200`; in another `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` returns 200 or 307, and `curl -k https://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` returns 200 with initialized=true/sealed=false (REQ-002)

Verify the exact Service name in the rendered openbao chart (`kubectl get svc -n openbao-system`) so the Quick Start command points at svc/openbao (client), not svc/openbao-internal (Raft peer discovery). Capture the svc name and port for use in the Quick Start (REQ-003)

Verify the root-token login path end-to-end: extract token with the documented one-liner, POST to /v1/auth/token/lookup-self, confirm policies=['root'] and that `bao kv list openstack/` reports keystone-admin, keystone-db (REQ-002)

Add a new numbered step to docs/quick-start.md (between current Step 4 Headlamp and Step 5 Deploy the Keystone operator) titled 'Step 4b — Open the OpenBao UI' (or a renumbered equivalent) containing: the svc/openbao port-forward command, the root-token one-liner, the https://localhost:8200/ui/ URL, the self-signed-cert warning, and a link to the bootstrap reference (REQ-003, REQ-004)

Update the 'Architecture snapshot' ::: details What gets deployed block around docs/quick-start.md:131 to list the OpenBao UI on svc/openbao :8200 in namespace openbao-system as a kind-only demo surface, matching Headlamp's phrasing (REQ-005)

Add a short cross-link in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md near the 'Running the Full Bootstrap' / root-token block (~lines 164-171) pointing back at the new Quick Start step; do NOT duplicate the port-forward/token/warning content (REQ-006)

Proofread all doc changes in-browser/in-IDE rendering: verify callout syntax (::: tip / > **Note:**) matches existing Quick Start callouts, that the feature-ID CC-0082 is cited in each new doc section, and that link anchors into the bootstrap reference resolve (REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)

Re-run `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/` against the kind cluster from task 2.1 and confirm it still passes unmodified (OpenBao StatefulSet ready, ExternalSecrets SecretSynced, ClusterSecretStore Valid) (REQ-008)

Exercise the chaos unseal flow: run `tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh` (or its equivalent chaos chainsaw suite) against the cluster and confirm it unseals with the existing 3-of-5 threshold and no new errors in pod logs (REQ-008)

Capture the curl-based UI smoke-check (port-forward + `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` + token lookup-self) as evidence in the PR description, so reviewers can reproduce the acceptance check without inventing commands (REQ-002)

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer is protected against accidentally enabling the UI in production
test_specifications.expected: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base` output, filtered to the openbao HelmRelease, contains `ui = true` and still contains the listener/storage/service_registration blocks. Tier: [unit] (manifest rendering).

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer is protected against accidentally enabling the UI in production
test_specifications.expected: `kustomize build deploy/flux-system` output, filtered to the openbao HelmRelease, still contains `ui = false` inside ha.raft.config; chart version pin unchanged. Tier: [unit] (manifest rendering).

test_specifications.story: CI keeps passing: infra-stack-health, chaos unseal, and deploy-infra all green
test_specifications.expected: After make deploy-infra on kind with the UI flip, OpenBao StatefulSet readyReplicas >= 1, ExternalSecrets keystone-admin/keystone-db/mariadb-root-password all Ready=True reason=SecretSynced, ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store Ready=True reason=Valid. Tier: [e2e].

test_specifications.story: Developer explores OpenBao KV paths and auth mounts from the browser in kind
test_specifications.expected: With `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200` running, `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` returns HTTP 200 or 307. Tier: [e2e] (manual smoke check, captured in PR body).

test_specifications.story: CI keeps passing: infra-stack-health, chaos unseal, and deploy-infra all green
test_specifications.expected: With port-forward running, `curl -k https://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` returns HTTP 200 JSON with initialized=true and sealed=false. Tier: [e2e] (manual smoke check).

test_specifications.story: Developer explores OpenBao KV paths and auth mounts from the browser in kind
test_specifications.expected: POST to /v1/auth/token/lookup-self using the token extracted from openbao-init-keys returns HTTP 200 with policies=['root']. Tier: [e2e] (manual smoke check).

test_specifications.story: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
test_specifications.expected: docs/quick-start.md contains a step with: (1) the exact `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200` command, (2) the root-token extraction one-liner matching openbao-bootstrap.md:170-171, (3) https://localhost:8200/ui/, (4) a self-signed-cert warning note. Tier: [unit] (doc-review checklist).

test_specifications.story: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
test_specifications.expected: The Quick Start step links to a specific anchor in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md for deeper KV/auth/policy material, instead of duplicating it. Tier: [unit] (doc-review checklist).

test_specifications.story: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
test_specifications.expected: The Quick Start step contains a note that explicitly names the self-signed-cert condition, identifies the internal cert-manager selfsigned-cluster-issuer as the issuer, tells the reader to click through in a throwaway browser profile, and does NOT instruct them to disable TLS verification system-wide or install the CA in their system trust store. Tier: [unit] (doc-review checklist).

test_specifications.story: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
test_specifications.expected: The ::: details What gets deployed block in docs/quick-start.md lists the OpenBao UI on svc/openbao :8200 as a kind-only demo surface, in the same phrasing style used for Headlamp. Tier: [unit] (doc-review checklist).

test_specifications.story: Quick Start reader follows a UI access step analogous to the Headlamp step
test_specifications.expected: docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md near the 'Running the Full Bootstrap' block (lines 164-171) contains a cross-link to the new Quick Start UI step; no duplicated walkthrough content. Tier: [unit] (doc-review checklist).

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer is protected against accidentally enabling the UI in production
test_specifications.expected: `git diff main -- deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml` is empty on the PR. Tier: [unit] (diff check).

test_specifications.story: CI keeps passing: infra-stack-health, chaos unseal, and deploy-infra all green

review_criteria: BLOCKER: deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml line 42 reads `ui = true` and no other HCL line in the standalone config block has been reflowed or altered (REQ-001).
BLOCKER: deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml shows an empty git diff — ui = false at line 43 unchanged, chart version pin `>=0.5.0 <1.0.0` at line 23 unchanged, no values edits (REQ-007).

BLOCKER: After make deploy-infra on a fresh kind cluster, `curl -k -I https://localhost:8200/ui/` returns HTTP 200 or 307, and `curl -k https://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` returns HTTP 200 JSON with initialized=true, sealed=false (REQ-002).
BLOCKER: tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml still passes without modification on the kind cluster with the UI flip (REQ-008).
BLOCKER: tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh still completes successfully with no new errors in pod logs (REQ-008).

CRITICAL: docs/quick-start.md contains a new step between current Step 4 and Step 5 with all four required elements — exact port-forward command on svc/openbao, exact token-extraction one-liner, https://localhost:8200/ui/ URL (HTTPS), and a self-signed-cert warning note that is specific and scoped (REQ-003, REQ-004).
CRITICAL: The Quick Start step links to a specific anchor in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md instead of duplicating the KV/auth/policy material (REQ-003).
CRITICAL: The self-signed-cert warning does NOT tell readers to disable TLS verification system-wide or install the internal CA into their system trust store (REQ-004).
CRITICAL: docs/quick-start.md architecture snapshot block around line 131 lists the OpenBao UI as a kind-only surface in the same phrasing style as Headlamp (REQ-005).

CRITICAL: docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md near the 'Running the Full Bootstrap' block (~lines 164-171) gains a short cross-link back to the new Quick Start UI step; no content duplication (REQ-006).
MINOR: The kind-overlay patch has an inline comment near `ui = true` referencing Feature ID CC-0082 and clarifying the kind-only scope (mirroring headlamp.yaml:12-15 comment framing).
MINOR: The Quick Start notes that svc/openbao is the client ClusterIP service (not svc/openbao-internal, which is the Raft peer-discovery headless svc).
MINOR: Each new doc section cites the Feature ID CC-0082 in line with critical-rule #11 (feature-id-required).
MINOR: make deploy-infra still completes within the existing HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT and POD_TIMEOUT budgets — no new wait step added.
implementation_notes:
Architecture and scope — The feature is a one-line HCL config toggle plus three documentation edits. The intentional scope boundary is: kind overlay only, docs only; no production flag change, no chart upgrade, no Ingress/LoadBalancer expansion, no new listener, no new service, no new RBAC. This keeps the blast radius to a single line of config and three docs files.

Architecture sketch (kind overlay only):

  ┌──────────────┐   kubectl port-forward
  │  developer   │───────────────────────┐
  │  workstation │                       │
  └──────────────┘                       ▼
                              ┌─────────────────────┐
                              │ svc/openbao :8200   │  (ClusterIP; client svc from chart)
                              │  namespace:         │
                              │  openbao-system     │
                              └─────────┬───────────┘
                                        │ TLS (openbao-tls from cert-manager)
                                        ▼
                              ┌─────────────────────┐
                              │   openbao-0 pod     │
                              │   (standalone mode  │
                              │    in kind overlay) │
                              │                     │
                              │  config.hcl:        │
                              │    ui = true  ◄───── ONLY THIS LINE CHANGES
                              │    listener tcp     │
                              │    storage raft     │
                              │    svc_registr. k8s │
                              └─────────────────────┘

Data flow (UI login, kind-only):
  1. Developer runs `kubectl port-forward -n openbao-system svc/openbao 8200:8200`.
  2. Browser opens https://localhost:8200/ui/ — warns about self-signed cert from selfsigned-cluster-issuer — developer clicks through in a throwaway profile.
  3. Developer extracts root token via `kubectl get secret openbao-init-keys ... | jq -r '.root_token'`.
  4. Developer pastes token into UI login → root session.
  5. UI renders the KV v2 mount (openstack/) with keystone-admin, keystone-db, mariadb-root-password; Auth Methods shows kubernetes/management mount.

Pitfalls to avoid:
  - Do NOT point port-forward at svc/openbao-internal — that's the headless Raft peer-discovery svc. Use svc/openbao (client ClusterIP).
  - Do NOT tell readers to disable TLS verification globally or install the internal CA into their system trust store — the warning is expected and click-through is the right answer for a kind demo.
  - Do NOT duplicate the UI walkthrough in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md; keep the Quick Start as the single source of truth.
  - Do NOT modify deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml, including the chart version pin at line 23.
  - Do NOT add a new listener/port — ui = true reuses the existing :8200 TLS listener.

Pattern files to follow:
  - docs/quick-start.md:150-170 for the step structure (wait → token → port-forward → URL → sidebar/description).
  - deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml:12-15 for the kind-only scope comment framing.
  - docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md:170-171 for the canonical token-extraction one-liner.

All doc and config references to this work must cite the Feature ID CC-0082 (critical-rule #11).
